mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models. Key trade-offs include complexity in setup and management versus high throughput and low latency.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the llm-d command-line interface to manage deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

llm-d version

```

> Download the latest CLI version.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.
> Verify the installation by checking the version.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes to ensure they are in 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install llm-d
steps.description:
Deploy llm-d to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.6.0 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Add the llm-d Helm repository.
> Install llm-d in the llm-d namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for llm-d.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
Remove llm-d from the cluster and warn about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

kubectl delete namespace llm-d

```

> Uninstall llm-d from the specified namespace.
> Delete the llm-d namespace, which may lead to data loss.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Backup existing configurations, upgrade llm-d, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.6.0 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup current llm-d configurations.
> Upgrade llm-d to the latest version.
> Verify that the llm-d pods are running.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Inspect logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust configurations or resource requests.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is not reachable or is overloaded.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster status with 'kubectl get nodes' and ensure the API server is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or add more nodes to the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new accelerators and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy configuration flags for model serving.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update configuration files to align with the new schema introduced in this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
